result.markdown:
---
title: Data Wrangling I
subtitle: "Manipulating data frames with `dplyr`"
format: stat198slides-revealjs
---

# Room for Improvement in Base R

##


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars <- data.frame(
    name = c("Anakin", "Padme", "Luke", "JarJar"),
    homeworld = c("Tatooine", "Naboo", "Tatooine", "Naboo"),
    height = c(1.8, 1.6, 1.7, 1.9),
    weight = c(84, 45, 77, 90)
)
```
:::


Using base R data frame subsetting, create from `star_wars`...

1. The vector of heights.
1. A data frame that contains only the characters shorter than 1.75.
1. A data frame that contains the four original cols plus the one with the height in inches.
1. The average height of characters from Tatooine

{{< countdown "2:30" >}}

## What's going on here?


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars[star_wars$height < 1.75, ]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
   name homeworld height weight
2 Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3  Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
```


:::
:::


. . .



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars$height
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 1.8 1.6 1.7 1.9
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars$height < 1.75
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE
```


:::
:::


## Comparison operators

You can form a logical vector through a logical comparison:

:::{.notes}
:::{.columns}

:::{.column width=50%}
`<`\
`>`\
`<=`\
`>=`\
:::

:::{.column width=50%}
`==`\
`!=`\
`%in%`\
:::

:::

:::

## Examples


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
c("apple", "orange", "apple", "kiwi") == "apple"
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
"apple" == c("apple", "orange", "apple", "kiwi")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE
```


:::
:::


. . .

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
"apple" %in% c("apple", "orange", "apple", "kiwi")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] TRUE
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
c("apple", "orange", "apple", "kiwi") %in% "apple"
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE
```


:::
:::


## Examples


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
c("apple", "kiwi") %in% c("apple", "orange", "apple", "kiwi")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] TRUE TRUE
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
c("apple", "orange", "apple", "kiwi") %in% c("apple", "kiwi")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE
```


:::
:::



## Let's make this better

:::{style="font-size:1.3em"}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars[star_wars$height < 1.75, ]
```
:::

:::

. . .

\
\


:::{style="font-size:1.3em"}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars$height_in <- star_wars$height * 39.3701
```
:::

:::

. . .

\
\

:::{style="font-size:1.3em"}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
mean(star_wars$height[star_wars$homeworld == "Tatooine"])
```
:::

:::


##

:::{.columns}

:::{.column width=50%}
![](images/tidy-manifesto.png)
:::

:::{.column width=50%}
Four Principles:

1. Reuse existing data structures.
   - The Data Frame
1. Compose simple functions with the pipe.
   - `|>`
1. Embrace functional programming.
   - Clear separate of data structure and functions.
1. Design for humans.
   - It should be readable!

:::

:::


## {auto-animate=true}

![](images/tidyverse-hex.svg){fig-align=center}


## {auto-animate=true}

:::{.columns}

:::{.column width=50%}
![](images/tidyverse-hex.svg)
:::

:::{.column width=50%}
![](images/dplyr-hex.svg)
:::
:::

# dplyr

## dplyr verbs

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width="50%"}
- `slice()`
- `select()`
- `filter()`
- `mutate()`
- `arrange()`
- `summarize()`
- `group_by()`
:::
:::{.column .fragment width="50%"}
1. All of these have a data frame as the first argument.
1. All of these produce a data frame as output.
1. Within the function, you can simply refer to column names unquoted.
:::

:::


## 

Load the package.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
```
:::


. . .

Our example data set.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::



## `slice()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .

Isolates particular rows of a data frame by row number.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column .fragment width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
slice(star_wars, 2)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
   name homeworld height weight
1 Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `slice()` {auto-animate=true}

Isolates particular rows of a data frame by row number.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
slice(star_wars, 2:4)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
3 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::
:::


## `slice()` {auto-animate=true}

Isolates particular rows of a data frame by row number.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45

4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```

:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
slice(star_wars, -3)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::
:::


## `select()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .

Selects variables by name or number.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
select(star_wars, homeworld)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  homeworld
1  Tatooine
2     Naboo
3  Tatooine
4     Naboo
```


:::
:::



## `select()` {auto-animate=true}

Selects variables by name or number.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
select(star_wars, 2)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  homeworld
1  Tatooine
2     Naboo
3  Tatooine
4     Naboo
```


:::
:::



## `select()` {auto-animate=true}

Selects variables by name or number.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
select(star_wars, homeworld, name)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  homeworld   name
1  Tatooine Anakin
2     Naboo  Padme
3  Tatooine   Luke
4     Naboo JarJar
```


:::
:::



## `filter()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .

Returns rows that meet certain criteria.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column .fragment width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
filter(star_wars, homeworld == "Naboo")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `filter()` {auto-animate=true}

Returns rows that meet certain criteria.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
filter(star_wars, weight < 50)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
   name homeworld height weight
1 Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `filter()` {auto-animate=true}

Returns rows that meet certain criteria.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
filter(star_wars, name %in% c("Padme", "Luke"))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
   name homeworld height weight
1 Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2  Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `filter()` {auto-animate=true}

Returns rows that meet certain criteria.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
filter(star_wars,
       name %in% c("Padme", "Luke"),
       weight < 50)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
   name homeworld height weight
1 Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

. . .

> You can add multiple conditions separated by `,`

## `mutate()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .

Adds a new variable that can be a function of previous variables.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
mutate(star_wars, weight_g = weight * 1000)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight weight_g
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84    84000
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45    45000
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77    77000
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90    90000
```


:::
:::




## `arrange()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .

Sort the rows of a data frame by the values of variables.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column .fragment width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
arrange(star_wars, height)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
3 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `arrange()` {auto-animate=true}

Sort the rows of a data frame by the values of variables.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
arrange(star_wars, desc(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
2 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `arrange()` {auto-animate=true}

Sort the rows of a data frame by the values of variables.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
arrange(star_wars, homeworld)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
3 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
4   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `arrange()` {auto-animate=true}

Sort the rows of a data frame by the values of variables.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
arrange(star_wars, homeworld, height)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
2 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
```


:::
:::

:::
:::


## `summarize()` {auto-animate=true}

. . .

Summarize a variable with a statistic.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summarize(star_wars,
          mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  mean(height)
1         1.75
```


:::
:::



## `summarize()` {auto-animate=true}

Summarize a variable with a statistic.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summarize(star_wars,
          avg_height = mean(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  avg_height
1       1.75
```


:::
:::



## `summarize()` {auto-animate=true}

Summarize a variable with a statistic.


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
summarize(star_wars,
          avg_height = mean(height),
          sd_height  = sd(height))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  avg_height sd_height
1       1.75 0.1290994
```


:::
:::


. . .

> Can calculate multiple statistics separated by `,`

##


::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars <- data.frame(
    name = c("Anakin", "Padme", "Luke", "JarJar"),
    homeworld = c("Tatooine", "Naboo", "Tatooine", "Naboo"),
    height = c(1.8, 1.6, 1.7, 1.9),
    weight = c(84, 45, 77, 90)
)
```
:::


Using **`dplyr`**, create from `star_wars`...

1. The vector of heights.
1. A data frame that contains only the characters shorter than 1.75.
1. A data frame that contains the four original cols plus the one with the height in inches.
1. The average height of characters from Tatooine

{{< countdown "3:00" >}}


# Data Wrangling Made Even Easier

## `select()` {auto-animate=true}

Selects variables by name or number.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```


:::
:::

:::

:::{.column .fragment width=50%}

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
select(star_wars, name:height)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9
```


:::
:::

:::
:::

## `select()` {auto-animate=true}

Selects variables by name or number.

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width=50%}

::: {.cell output-location='default'}

```{.r .cell-code}
star_wars
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
    name homeworld height weight
1 Anakin  Tatooine    1.8     84
2  Padme     Naboo    1.6     45
3   Luke  Tatooine    1.7     77
4 JarJar     Naboo    1.9     90
```

<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
